page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-144 is a high-density compute server built on the x86_64 architecture. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor featuring 72 physical cores and 144 virtual cores via hyperthreading, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 540 GB of dedicated RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 3.75 GB of memory per core. The processor features a substantial cache hierarchy, including 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 504 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage, bundled GPUs, or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external network storage attachment for persistent data.

Benchmark evaluations place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory subsystem performance. It ranks in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU throughput, PassMark CPU scores, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. The memory architecture delivers exceptional bandwidth, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 11548.62 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 213.92 GB/sec. The integration of advanced hardware instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, enables superior execution speeds for cryptographic operations, vector calculations, and software compilation tasks.

This server type is highly optimized for compute-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale database operations, and high-throughput web serving. It achieves 212.01 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and delivers 220.56 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. The hardware-accelerated matrix extensions also make it highly capable for CPU-based artificial intelligence tasks, demonstrating top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language models. The lack of local storage and GPU hardware positions this instance as a dedicated, high-density compute node for distributed analytics, ETL pipelines, and parallel compilation workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-144 is a high-density compute server built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 72 physical cores and 144 vCPUs. It is configured with 540 GB of dedicated RAM, yielding 3.75 GB per core, alongside a 504 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations, aided by hardware-accelerated AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. It delivers exceptional throughput for Redis operations and static web serving. This server is highly optimized for CPU-intensive workloads, including software compilation, database management, and large language model prompt processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-144 is a high-density server featuring 144 vCPUs, 540 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-144 features 144 vCPUs, 540 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier multi-core compute performance.
tagline: A high-density, 144-vCPU compute instance featuring advanced Intel AMX matrix acceleration and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 144 vCPUs and 540 GB RAM
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing
Optimized for high-throughput database operations and static web serving
Well-suited for CPU-based LLM prompt processing and software compilation
